summary:
The superior rectal artery supplies the upper anal canal above the pectinate line; the middle rectal artery covers the proximal portion near its transition point; and the inferior rectal artery provides blood flow to the lower anal canal, including the sphincters and skin beneath the pectinate line. Together, these three arteries fully cover the anal canal through distinct regional contributions.

Only include information from Agent1’s response. Do NOT add outside knowledge or commentary.

The superior, middle, and inferior rectal arteries respectively supply the upper, proximal, and lower portions of the anal canal above and below the pectinate line. All three vessels collectively provide complete vascularization of the anal canal.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$
